metadata.title: WCH CH32V203G6U6
metadata.description: HAIR extraction for the WCH CH32V203G6U6 using the approved WCH datasheet/reference materials, official openwch SDK assets, and exact-family/variant structured cross-checks.

evidence.name: Datasheet scope metadata
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official WCH CH32V203DS0 datasheet v3.0 explicitly covers CH32V203G6U6 as part of the CH32V203 family model table.

evidence.name: Model comparison table
evidence.normalizedClaim: CH32V203G6U6 provides 32KB flash, 10KB SRAM, 24 GPIOs, 2 USART/UART, 1 SPI, 1 I2C, 1 CAN, USBD present, USBHD absent, and two 10-channel ADC/TKey units.

evidence.name: Architecture overview
evidence.normalizedClaim: CH32V203 is based on the QingKe V4B 32-bit RISC-V core and supports up to 144MHz system frequency.

evidence.name: Clock and supply facts
evidence.normalizedClaim: The device uses 2.4V to 3.6V system supply, built-in 8MHz HSI RC, built-in 40KHz LSI RC, external 3MHz to 25MHz HSE, external 32.768KHz LSE, and PLL-derived SYSCLK up to 144MHz.

evidence.name: Memory map figure
evidence.normalizedClaim: The family memory map places SRAM at 0x20000000, APB/AHB peripheral space at 0x40000000, and USBFS at 0x50000000.

evidence.name: Package and ordering table
evidence.normalizedClaim: CH32V203G6U6 is the exact QFN28 package variant in the CH32V203 family model table and package pin-definition table.

evidence.name: G6U6 OSC pin remap note
evidence.normalizedClaim: For CH32V203G6U6, the OSC_IN and OSC_OUT package pads can be remapped to GPIO PD0 and PD1 by software.

evidence.name: Generic D6 linker memory layout
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official openwch generic D6 linker script documents the low-density CH32V203F6/G6/C6 memory layout as flash at the 0x00000000 boot alias with 32KB length and RAM at 0x20000000 with 10KB length.

evidence.name: Header D6 grouping
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official header maps CH32V20x_D6 to low/medium-density CH32V203 parts including CH32V203G6 class devices.

evidence.name: Peripheral base macros
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official header defines the CH32V20x D6 peripheral base addresses used for the structural peripheral map.

evidence.name: IRQ enumeration
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official header enumerates the CH32V20x_D6 interrupt identifiers used by the G6U6 variant.

evidence.name: Startup vector table
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official D6 startup file defines the G6U6-class external interrupt vector ordering from WWDG through DMA1_Channel8.

evidence.name: Reference manual overview
evidence.normalizedClaim: The CH32FV2x_V3xRM reference manual is the official register and peripheral manual for CH32V203G6U6-class devices.

evidence.name: QingKe V4 processor scope
evidence.normalizedClaim: The QingKe V4 processor manual covers V4A/V4B/V4C/V4F cores and provides ISA-level details for the CH32V203G6U6 QingKe V4B core.

evidence.name: SDK compiler architecture flags
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official CH32V203G6U6 example build compiles for -march=rv32imacxw and -mabi=ilp32, with no floating-point ISA extension enabled.

evidence.name: PFIC priority bit layout
evidence.normalizedClaim: The CH32V20x core header documents interrupt priority using bit7 as pre-emption priority and bits6-bit4 as subpriority, implying four implemented interrupt priority bits.

evidence.name: Vendor system timer block
evidence.normalizedClaim: The CH32V20x core support code exposes a vendor-defined SysTick register block at 0xE000F000 and the official CH32V203G6U6 debug support code configures it directly for delay timing, so the device uses vendor system-timer configuration rather than a CMSIS-standard SysTick abstraction.

locator.fragment: C++/Use MRS Create C++ project-example/CH32V203G6U6++/Core/core_riscv.h and Debug/debug.c

evidence.name: QFN28 pin definition table
evidence.normalizedClaim: Table 3-1-3 defines the CH32V203 QFN28 package pad assignments, default functions, and alternate/remap functions used for the CH32V203G6U6 pin model.

evidence.name: Vendor header register structs
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official CH32V20x header defines typed register structs, member order, widths, and offsets for the major CH32V203G6U6 peripheral families, including USB, CAN, DMA channels, and timers.

evidence.name: Vendor header bit-definition sections
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official CH32V20x header provides register bit masks and bit-position macros for ADC, AFIO, BKP, CAN core registers, CRC, DMA, EXTI, FLASH, GPIO, I2C, IWDG, PWR, RCC, RTC, SPI, TIM, USART, WWDG, and related blocks.

evidence.name: RM TIM1 advanced timer register model
evidence.normalizedClaim: The reference manual models TIM1 as an advanced-control timer on CH32V20x_D6, with RPTCR, BDTR, DMACFGR, and DMAADR in the TIM1 register block.

evidence.name: RM TIM2-TIM4 register tables
evidence.normalizedClaim: The CH32V20x_D6 TIM2, TIM3, and TIM4 register tables omit BDTR and RPTCR, retain DMACFGR and DMAADR, and widen TIM2 CNT/ATRLR/CCR1-4 to 32 bits.

evidence.name: RM TIMx_AUX availability note
evidence.normalizedClaim: The TIMx_AUX register is only available for selected CH32F20x_D8 / CH32V30x_D8 / CH32V31x_D8C products, excluding CH32V20x_D6 devices such as CH32V203G6U6.

evidence.name: RM CAN1 filter-bank extent
evidence.normalizedClaim: The CH32V20x CAN1 filter register table extends from F0R1/F0R2 through F27R1/F27R2, so CH32V203G6U6 exposes 28 filter banks even though the header bit-definition sections stop at bank 13.

evidence.name: Vendor header USB register structs
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official CH32V20x header defines concrete USBFS device and USBFS host register structs at the shared USBFS base address, establishing register presence and offsets even though matching bit-definition sections are absent.

evidence.name: Vendor header register window extents
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official CH32V20x header's typed peripheral structs, nested mailbox/filter structs, and reserved padding bound the contiguous register-window extent of each modeled peripheral block, allowing address-block coverage to be derived from the struct layout.

evidence.name: Core access qualifier definitions
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official CH32V20x core header defines __I as read-only, __O as write-only, and __IO as read/write, providing the access-permission mapping used by the peripheral access-layer structs.

evidence.name: Community CH32V203 SVD metadata overlap
evidence.normalizedClaim: The community CH32V203xx SVD provides register descriptions, many register reset values, field descriptions, and some field access annotations for CH32V203-family peripherals. When those metadata are imported only on exact peripheral-name, register-name-plus-offset, and field-name-plus-bit-range matches against the official-source-derived HAIR topology, they serve as an auditable metadata gap-filler without reshaping the recovered register structure.

evidence.name: Community CH32V203G6U6 YAML exact-variant fit
evidence.normalizedClaim: The community CH32V203G6U6 YAML names the exact CH32V203G6U6 variant, records the QFN28 package and 32KB flash / 10KB RAM sizing, and includes CH32V203 peripheral fragments such as USART1/2, TIM1, TIM2/3/4, ADC1/2, SPI1, I2C1, USBD, and CAN1 for conservative exact-variant topology reuse.

evidence.name: Community CH32V203G6U6 USBD register fragment
evidence.normalizedClaim: The pinned CH32V203G6U6 ch32-data source includes the USBD peripheral fragment, which resolves to the shared USB register definitions and supports conservative device-mode register metadata reuse without importing the absent USBFS host overlay.

evidence.name: Community CH32V203G6U6 clock/reset topology
evidence.normalizedClaim: The pinned CH32V203G6U6 YAML and its included family/peripheral fragments identify the HCLK, PCLK1, PCLK2, PCLK1_TIM, PCLK2_TIM, and ADC clock domains plus the RCC enable/reset register bindings for DMA1, GPIOA-D, AFIO, USART1/2, SPI1, I2C1, TIM1/2/3/4, ADC1/2, CAN1, and USBD.

locator.fragment: CH32V203G6U6.yaml -> ../family/CH32V2.yaml and included peripheral YAML rcc sections

evidence.name: Community CH32V203G6U6 interrupt topology
evidence.normalizedClaim: The pinned CH32V203G6U6 YAML and the included CH32V2_D6 interrupt map define the interrupt signal to vector bindings for DMA1 channels, ADC1/2, USART1/2, SPI1, I2C1, TIM1/2/3/4, CAN1, USBD shared lines, and the common RCC/EXTI/RTC family signals for this subgroup; absent peripherals stay pruned from the exact-variant extraction.

locator.fragment: CH32V203G6U6.yaml -> ../interrupts/CH32V2_D6.yaml and included peripheral YAML interrupt sections

evidence.name: Community CH32V203G6U6 DMA channel map
evidence.normalizedClaim: The pinned CH32V203G6U6 YAML includes the CH32V_V4B DMA map assigning DMA1 channels to ADC1, USART1/2, SPI1, I2C1, and timer request lines for the QingKe V4B family.

evidence.name: Community CH32V203G6U6 pin and remap topology
evidence.normalizedClaim: The pinned CH32V203G6U6 peripheral YAML fragments enumerate USART, SPI, I2C, TIM, ADC, CAN, and USBD signal-to-pin routes and the AFIO remap controls that select alternate routes; routes landing on pins absent from the QFN28 package must be excluded for the exact variant.

locator.fragment: CH32V203G6U6.yaml -> included peripheral YAML pin/remap sections

evidence.name: Vendor header TIM counter enable bit
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official CH32V20x header defines TIM_CEN as the CTLR1 counter-enable control bit for the general and advanced timer blocks.

evidence.name: Vendor header ADC enable and calibration bits
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official CH32V20x header defines ADC_ADON, ADC_CAL, and ADC_RSTCAL in CTLR2, establishing the documented enable and calibration controls used by ADC1 and ADC2.

evidence.name: Official ADC1 DMA example
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official WCH ADC_DMA example enables ADC1 DMA, configures DMA1_Channel1 with DMA_DIR_PeripheralSRC from ADC1->RDATAR to memory, and waits for DMA1 transfer complete on channel 1.

evidence.name: Official TIM1 update DMA example
evidence.normalizedClaim: The official WCH TIM_DMA example initializes DMA1_Channel5 with DMA_DIR_PeripheralDST targeting TIM1_CH1CVR_ADDRESS, enables TIM_DMA_Update on TIM1, and uses that route to update PWM output values.

evidence.name: Adafruit USB-C board usage
evidence.normalizedClaim: Adafruit documents the QT Py CH32V203 USB-C port as used for both powering and programming the board.

evidence.name: Adafruit native USB device note
evidence.normalizedClaim: Adafruit describes the board as exposing the CH32V203 native USB device interface and notes USB CDC support via TinyUSB at board level.

evidence.name: Adafruit schematic USB nets and separate UART nets
evidence.normalizedClaim: The Adafruit schematic routes the USB-C D+ and D- nets directly to IC2 USBD-labeled pins, while the board's RX and TX nets are separately routed to PA3 and PA2; no discrete USB-UART bridge is shown.

evidence.name: Adafruit schematic USB-C CC termination
evidence.normalizedClaim: The Adafruit schematic places 5.1 kOhm resistors on both USB-C CC1 and CC2, consistent with a device-side sink attachment on the USB-C receptacle.

evidence.name: Adafruit board UART header roles
evidence.normalizedClaim: Adafruit documents the board UART header as RX on PA3 and TX on PA2.

evidence.name: Adafruit board NeoPixel pinout
evidence.normalizedClaim: Adafruit documents the onboard RGB NeoPixel LED as connected to PA4.

evidence.name: Adafruit schematic NeoPixel net
evidence.normalizedClaim: The Adafruit schematic names the onboard RGB LED data net NEOPIX and routes it to IC2 pin PA4.

provenance.notes: The datasheet maps the underlying code flash at 0x08000000 and describes 0x00000000 as the boot alias; the official generic D6 linker script uses that alias and documents the 32KB/10KB low-density layout for CH32V203F6/G6/C6.

provenance.notes: The header exposes a shared OPA control block at OPA_BASE; this rerun does not split OPA1 and OPA2 into separate semantic entities.

adafruitQtPyCh32V203.notes: Board collateral shows USB-C attached to the native USB device interface and not to a discrete USB-UART bridge. The exact D+/D- pad labels in the Adafruit schematic conflict with the WCH exact-variant package map, so the authoritative MCU USB pin routes remain the WCH-backed PA11/PA12 routes already modeled elsewhere in this document.

provenance.notes: Register presence and offsets come from the vendor header structs; descriptions and field metadata are conservatively imported from the pinned community ch32-data USB register fragments reachable from CH32V203G6U6.yaml while preserving the device-only USB topology used by this exact variant.

fields.name: DMA_EN
fields.description: DMA enable and DMA interrupt enable for USB.

fields.name: CLR_ALL
fields.description: force clear FIFO and count of USB.

fields.name: RESET_SIE
fields.description: force reset USB SIE, need software clear.

fields.name: INT_BUSY
fields.description: enable automatic responding busy for device mode or automatic pause for host mode during interrupt flag UIF_TRANSFER valid.

fields.name: SYS_CTRL
fields.description: USB device enable and internal pullup resistance enable.

fields.name: HOST_MODE
fields.description: enable USB host mode: 0=device mode, 1=host mode.

interruptControllers.name: PFIC
interruptControllers.description: Fast programmable interrupt controller integrated with the QingKe V4B core.

pins.name: VSS
pins.description: Digital ground pin group used by the exposed VSS pad and the dedicated VSS package pad.

pins.name: PD0
pins.description: Defaults to OSC_IN after reset on CH32V203G6U6 and can be remapped to GPIO PD0 by software.

pins.name: PD1
pins.description: Defaults to OSC_OUT after reset on CH32V203G6U6 and can be remapped to GPIO PD1 by software.

packages.name: QFN28
packages.description: QFN28 package used by CH32V203G6U6, including the exposed VSS pad shown as pad 0 in the datasheet pin-definition table.

steps.description: Set CTLR1.CEN to enable the counter.
Set CTLR1.CEN to enable the counter.
Set CTLR1.CEN to enable the counter.
Set CTLR1.CEN to enable the counter.
Divide the reset-default 8 MHz timer clock down to a 1 kHz timer tick.
Run the free-running counter across the full 16-bit range.
Start the time base from zero on initialization.
Initialize the compare register before arming alarms.
Apply the prescaler and reload configuration immediately.

provenance.notes: Repository-authored 1 kHz TIM4 time-base configuration for the generated Embassy async timing path. The PSC value assumes the reset-default 8 MHz timer clock used by the current CH32 smoke flow.

steps.description: Acknowledge the TIM4 compare-channel 1 interrupt flag.
Acknowledge the EXTI pending state for line 17.
Enable the A/D converter by setting CTLR2.ADON.
Reset the calibration logic by setting CTLR2.RSTCAL.
Start A/D calibration by setting CTLR2.CAL.
Enable the A/D converter by setting CTLR2.ADON.
Reset the calibration logic by setting CTLR2.RSTCAL.
Start A/D calibration by setting CTLR2.CAL.

states.name: disabled
states.description: CTLR1.CEN is cleared and the counter is stopped.

states.name: enabled
states.description: CTLR1.CEN is set and the counter runs.

effects.description: Counter starts when CEN is asserted.
Counter stops when CEN is cleared.

states.description: CTLR1.CEN is cleared and the counter is stopped.

states.name: enabled
states.description: CTLR1.CEN is set and the counter runs.

effects.description: Counter starts when CEN is asserted.
Counter stops when CEN is cleared.

provenance.notes: The family-level header and exact-variant YAML preserve the GPIOC register block, but the QFN28 package exposes no GPIOC pads.
The TIM4 UP sub-cause partition on the shared TIM4 vector is community-backed from ch32-rs rather than directly enumerated in the official WCH startup file.
The TIM4 TRG sub-cause partition on the shared TIM4 vector is community-backed from ch32-rs rather than directly enumerated in the official WCH startup file.
The TIM4 CC sub-cause partition on the shared TIM4 vector is community-backed from ch32-rs rather than directly enumerated in the official WCH startup file.
The TIM4 UP route shares one official TIM4 vector and uses the community ch32-rs sub-cause partition to model the update path explicitly.
The TIM4 TRG route shares one official TIM4 vector and uses the community ch32-rs sub-cause partition to model the trigger path explicitly.
Controller-level Embassy USB device driver. Higher-level USB classes such as CDC ACM are expected to come from layered Embassy USB libraries rather than from the HAIR driver contract itself.
The TIM4 CC route shares one official TIM4 vector and uses the community ch32-rs sub-cause partition to model the compare path explicitly.

Repository-authored RTC-backed Embassy async timing uses the CH32 RTC split counter/alarm registers and the dedicated RTC alarm interrupt path, with generated bring-up selecting the internal LSI source.
